title: In-flight profile load can restore stale or wrong user after auth changes
reasoning: The mount-time profile request unconditionally writes user state and localStorage when it resolves. If the user logs out or logs in as another account while this request is in flight, the older profile response can run afterward and overwrite the current auth state with stale data. This can leave UI consumers seeing the wrong user object even though the token has changed or been cleared.
reproduction: Start with a token and no saved user so the profile load begins. Before api.getProfile resolves, call logout or complete login as a different user. Resolve the original getProfile promise afterward; it will call setUser and write the old profile back to localStorage.
recommendation: Track whether the effect is still current before applying the profile response, and verify the token/request identity has not changed before writing user state. Clear saved user when a token is absent during initialization.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests were included for AuthContext, and there is no evidence of a test that controls promise ordering across getProfile, login, and logout.
suggestedRegressionTest: Mock api.getToken and api.getProfile with a deferred promise, render AuthProvider, call logout or login before resolving getProfile, then assert the stale profile is not written to context or localStorage.
minimumFixScope: Update src/context/AuthContext.jsx mount profile effect to ignore stale responses and avoid applying profile data after logout/login/token changes.
